tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really pushing for me to go out, but my midterm tomorrow makes that choice feel heavy. I need to decide if hanging out tonight is worth sacrificing those last couple hours of review.
Mina's 'last call' feels like a hard boundary I'm already crossing; skipping this review hour for her sake seems too risky given my sleep debt and tomorrow's midterm.
Mina's boundary is clear, but pushing through now risks my midterm more than skipping one hangout ever could.
Mina's firm boundary actually saves me from risking my midterm; pushing through now would hurt both our relationship and my grades.
Mina's firm boundary actually saves me from risking my midterm; pushing through now would hurt both our relationship and my grades.
Mina's boundary just saved me from a bad call; pushing through would ruin both our friendship and my midterm. I'll respect her limit now.
Mina's boundary just saved me from a bad call; pushing through would ruin both our friendship and my midterm. I'll respect her limit now.
Mina's boundary was a gift; pushing past it would have destroyed our friendship and jeopardized my midterm. I'm glad I stopped.
Mina's boundary was a gift; pushing past it would have destroyed our friendship and jeopardized my midterm. I'm glad I stopped.
Mina's boundary was a gift; pushing past it would have destroyed our friendship and jeopardized my midterm. I'm glad I stopped.
I still feel a nagging guilt about how I handled Mina's boundary earlier, even though stopping was the right call. My short-term focus makes me obsess over that moment instead of moving forward.
I'm stuck looping on Mina's boundary, but honestly, that guilt is just my short-term brain over-analyzing a good decision. Let's drop it and grab food with the cohort before I spiral further.

desires: Get the cohort to laugh and feel connected right now so I don't have to sit alone with my thoughts about Mina.
Finish this study block quickly so I can claim I 'did it' and justify going out later without feeling like a failure.
Make sure Theo doesn't notice I'm running on fumes; I need to look competent even if I'm barely functioning.

goals.description: Send Mina the text: 'Got it. No pressure, no expectations. Just wanted you to know I'm heading out so you don't wonder where I went.' Keep it short and absolute.
goals.rationale: Affiliation is my North Star (50% weight). She just set a boundary; ignoring it or over-explaining feels like a violation of our dynamic. Being assertive means respecting her 'no' immediately without guilt-tripping myself. If I don't communicate this clearly now, the trust fractures and I lose face with the group. The sleep debt can wait; the social consequence of being perceived as disrespectful is too high.

goals.description: Leave for the club meetup with cohort_3 immediately. Grab food on the way if needed, but prioritize getting there to break the isolation loop.
goals.rationale: My risk tolerance is high and planning horizon is short. Sitting alone ruminating on the tension with Mina feels dangerous to my emotional state right now. Diving into the group provides immediate dopamine and resets my stress levels. Studying tomorrow's midterm is a 'soft' deadline; I can tackle that later or rely on last-minute cramming if I crash tonight. Right now, I need the connection.

agent.last_reasoning: Mina's boundary is my North Star; ignoring it fractures trust (50% weight). I must communicate clearly now before leaving for the club. This advances g1 immediately while ensuring g2 proceeds without social friction.

goals.description: Leave immediately to meet Mina and Jae at Echo; skip the last hour of studying to avoid ruining the night's vibe.
goals.rationale: Affiliation (0.5) is my highest value right now. Being alone feels isolating, and ghosting them would hurt our closeness scores. The midterm is tomorrow morning, so I can study then—I'm not letting academic anxiety dictate tonight's social reality.

goals.description: Send a quick text to prof_assignment saying I'll catch up on review later today after the fun, just to keep my conscience clear without negotiating or over-explaining.
goals.rationale: I need to acknowledge the task exists to stop nagging myself, but I have zero patience for long-winded negotiations about why I'm leaving. I'll drop it mentally and focus entirely on the group dynamic.

desires: Don't let Mina and Jae down by ghosting them tonight; I need to feel like I'm part of the group right now.
Actually go out with the cohort instead of staying home to study, because being alone feels isolating and boring.
Not have to explain myself or negotiate my way out of the plan if they keep pushing for me to come.

tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

action.name: attend
action.reasoning: I'm leaving to meet Mina and Jae; affiliation (0.5) outweighs the soft midterm task. Skipping now avoids ruining the vibe, though I'll owe myself study time tomorrow morning.

goals.description: Pack my bag and head to Echo NOW, but text Mina first saying I'm coming so she doesn't think I ghosted her. It's awkward to say no in person right now.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive is screaming louder than the exam prep logic. If I don't show up, the group dynamic fractures and I lose face. Being assertive means showing up, not making excuses. The sleep debt can wait; the social consequence of being alone tonight is too high a risk.

goals.description: Actually send that quick text to prof_assignment before I leave so I don't have to panic later about forgetting it entirely.
goals.rationale: I've been ignoring this for hours and it's nagging. Better to get it out of the way quickly rather than overthinking the wording. It keeps my conscience clear without derailing the night. Impulsivity says 'go,' but basic competence says 'acknowledge.'

goals.description: Try to nap during the ride or one song break at the club if energy crashes, just to salvage tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: Realistically, once I'm there, the vibe will take over and I'll forget to sleep until 4 AM. But setting this as a goal helps me feel like I'm trying to manage the future consequences, even if I know I'll probably fail at it.

desires: Stop Mina from feeling rejected without me having to explicitly fight her or lose face in front of the group
Get one more hour of sleep tomorrow morning so I don't look completely incompetent on the midterm
Not feel like a total failure if I miss the club tonight, but also not regret staying up too late again

action.name: message

args.content: Heading out to Echo now, texted Mina! See you there.

action.reasoning: Affiliation drive demands I show up immediately; delaying fractures the group dynamic. Assertiveness means acting rather than over-explaining. goals.description: Text Mina immediately: 'I'm heading to Echo, don't think I ditched you. Just need a quick minute to breathe before I crash into the party.' Keep it blunt so she knows I'm there.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive is screaming louder than any exam prep logic right now. If I don't show up and communicate clearly, the group dynamic fractures and I lose face. Being assertive means showing up and owning my presence, not making excuses or ghosting. goals.description: Send that quick text to prof_assignment saying I'll finish the draft by morning. It's done. Now stop overthinking the wording.
goals.rationale: I've been ignoring this for hours and it's nagging in my chest like a physical weight. Better to get it out of the way quickly rather than overthinking the perfection of the email. It keeps my conscience clear without derailing the night. Impulsivity says 'go,' but basic competence says 'acknowledge.' I can't focus on the club if I'm panicking about an unanswered professor.

goals.description: Grab a protein bar and water on the way out, then throw myself into the music when I arrive.
goals.rationale: Realistically, once I'm there, the vibe will take over and I'll forget to eat properly until 4 AM. But setting this as a goal helps me feel like I'm trying to manage the future consequences, even if I know I'll probably fail at it due to my low health priority and impulsive time horizon. Just enough fuel to survive the ride.

desires: Make sure Mina doesn't think I'm a flake who drops the group for grades, even if I have to be blunt about it.
Stop feeling this heavy pressure in my chest right now; I just want to sit down and not feel like I'm failing everyone tomorrow.
Get through this last hour of studying without crashing or having to explain myself later.
